assumptions: Entity precision/recall uses a manually curated gold set with alias matching so hyphenation and snake_case variants are treated as the same concept.
Relation quality scores unordered meaningful pairs; relation type histograms are reported separately to show whether the LLM emits typed edges instead of only generic co-occurrence.
Query expansion compares three retrieval conditions: no linked entities, only LLM-extracted entities/relations, and an oracle manual-entity upper bound with shared topical seeds.

dataset.text: JWT rotation protects access tokens during mobile sign-in recovery using RS256
DuckDB hybrid search uses cosine similarity with HNSW indexing for vector retrieval
The pce_memory_activate handler validates scope and boundary before ranking
Git-based CRDT sync merges claims by content_hash with monotonic boundary upgrade
Redis cache stores rendered dashboard fragments with TTL-based eviction
OAuth device flow pins PKCE verifier state before token exchange with rotating refresh grants
pgvector semantic search blends BM25 reranking with approximate nearest-neighbor recall
SchemaDriftGuard blocks tenant rollout when policy-bound migration steps violate boundary rules
Replica repair replays vector clocks before gossip-based conflict resolution in offline sync
CDN edge cache invalidates persisted GraphQL responses after shard rebalancing

notes: Pattern NLP relations are synthetic co-occurs edges between all extracted entities in the same claim.
LLM relation quality improves only when the extractor emits typed edges that land on the gold relation pairs.

notes: The manual-entity arm adds one shared topical seed entity per claim cluster as an oracle upper bound for query expansion.
The LLM arm uses only entities and relations produced by the real Ollama-backed extractor on the claim text itself.
All three retrieval arms reuse the same deterministic local embedding service so score differences come from entity graph coverage, not embedding drift.
